subject: Chemistry
prerequisites: Particulate and macroscopic representations of chemical phenomena

learning_objectives.statement: Identify the components of a reaction mechanism.
A reaction mechanism consists of a series of elementary reactions, or steps, that occur in sequence. The components may include reactants, intermediates, products, and catalysts.
The elementary steps when combined should align with the overall balanced equation of a chemical reaction.
A reaction intermediate is produced by some elementary steps and consumed by others, such that it is present only while a reaction is occurring.
Experimental detection of a reaction intermediate is a common way to build evidence in support of one reaction mechanism over an alternative mechanism.

depth_profile: Students must understand that a reaction mechanism is a sequence of elementary steps that must sum to the overall balanced chemical equation. They must be able to identify the various components of a mechanism, specifically distinguishing between reactants, products, catalysts, and intermediates. Intermediates are explicitly defined as species produced in one step and consumed in a subsequent step. While students should know that experimental detection of intermediates supports a proposed mechanism, the actual collection of data pertaining to the detection of a reaction intermediate is explicitly excluded from the AP Exam.
assessment_notes: Students are assessed on their ability to describe the components of models and representations (Skill 1.B). They will likely be asked to identify intermediates and catalysts from a given multi-step mechanism, or to verify that the elementary steps add up to the overall balanced equation. A common task is distinguishing between an intermediate (produced then consumed) and a catalyst (consumed then produced). Questions will not require students to collect or analyze raw data regarding the experimental detection of intermediates.

spec_checklist.can_do: I can identify the components of a reaction mechanism.
I can recognize that a reaction mechanism consists of a series of elementary reactions or steps, which may include reactants, intermediates, products, and catalysts.
I can verify that the elementary steps of a mechanism combine to yield the overall balanced chemical equation.
I can identify a reaction intermediate as a species produced in one elementary step and consumed in another.
I can understand that detecting a reaction intermediate provides experimental evidence to support a proposed reaction mechanism.

spec_source_citation: AP Chemistry Course and Exam Description, V.1
